summary: Rob Madore stopped 31 shots and the Cincinnati Cyclones scored on two of three power-play chances Friday night to beat the Alaska Aces 3-1 at U.S. Bank Arena and square the series at two games apiece.

title: Ned Rozell: Lost and found: the glaciers of Attu Island
summary: In late August, seabird biologist John Piatt paused from his task of "stealing food from baby puffins" to look up at the green mountains of Attu, the last island in the long sweep of the Aleutians.

title: AK Beat: Wildfire ignites near Fairbanks
summary: Fire crews responded to a 7-acre wildfire that ignited east of Fairbanks Monday evening and burned within a mile of small subdivisions and the trans-Alaska pipeline, according to the state Division of Forestry.

title: Alaska fiscal year ends with only slight decline in oil production
summary: The state fiscal year ended Monday, with oil production for the year averaging more than 529,000 barrels a day, only slightly less than the preceding year and beating the projections offered by the state Department of Revenue earlier this year.

title: Nerd numbers alert: Ooh, the suspense around Lafranchise-MacKenzie
summary: If anyone cares -- and evidently, some do, though why is something of a puzzlement -- Alaska Aces defenseman Kane Lafranchise will wear No. 6 tonight in Game 3 against Stockton. Drew MacKenzie, who wears No. 4, the number Lafranchise wore for several seasons before spending most of this season in the AHL, will continue with that digit on his sweater. Also, winger Peter Sivak (upper-body injury) is out again. He must be since he was placed on reserve today. By DOYLE WOODY, adn.com Anchorage

title: Fireweed 400 registration ends Sunday
summary: The deadline to register for the Fireweed 400 bike race ends Sunday.

title: Aces beat Steelheads 5-2 on the road in Game 3
summary: The Aces received goals from five different players Monday and never trailed in securing a 5-2 road win over the Idaho Steelheads in Game 3 of the Western Conference semfinal series at CenturyLink Arena in Boise.

title: AK Beat: Big changes in health insurance rates in store for Palmer city employees and their dependents
summary: Higher costs, fewer fringe bennies: The City of Palmer will announce changes to its healthcare offerings for municipal employees at a meeting, Tuesday, at 6 p.m. The city mayor and administrator were mum on specifics.

title: Agency calls for Arctic offshore lease suggestions
summary: The Interior Department has put out a call for expressions of interest by the petroleum industry for expanded drilling in the Arctic Ocean.

title: AK Beat: 5.4 magnitude quake near Alaska's Kodiak Island
summary: A quake rattled near Kodiak Island late Friday night. Authorities say there is no tsunami danger.

title: Lumberjack act a fair favorite, year after year
summary: Fred Scheer's Lumberjack Show is as much a part of the Alaska State Fair as giant cabbages and decadent cream puffs, a fixture for more than 20 years. That's a lot of yo-hos.

title: Palin names Sullivan attorney general
summary: Gov. Sarah Palin today appointed Daniel S. Sullivan, a former assistant secretary of state in the administration of George W. Bush, to be Alaska's attorney general.

title: Judge delivers 'bittersweet' polar bear ruling
summary: A key section of an Interior Department rule has been thrown out that declared global warming threatening to the survival of polar bears.

title: 'I know I made a difference': A report from the frontlines of Alaska's fight against FASD
summary: The Daily News will publish the next two installments of our ongoing fetal alcohol syndrome coverage on Saturday and Sunday, including a look at how, despite success in reducing rates across Alaska, the state has missed many opportunities to combat the disability. Among the people featured in the story is Ellen Sovalik of Barrow. A former North Slope Borough health director and now a tribal judge, Sovalik was a newly sober 20-something when she left a janitor job to pioneer a boots-on-the-ground effort to teach expectant mothers about the danger of drinking while pregnant.

title: Amanda Coyne: School of hard knocks, sucker punches, and Colorado cigarettes
summary: Are advocates of changing our constitution to allow for vouchers getting a lesson in the school of hard knocks? "Kids not Cuts" signs are sprouting like weeds. Protests all across the state are being organized. Supporters are working the phones, hard.
